Member of Parliament for North Cotswolds (The Cotswolds 1997-2024 and for Cirencester & Tewkesbury 1992-97)

Farmer, Chartered Surveyor, and Chair of the Public Accounts Committee. Interests include the countryside, conservation, encouraging inward investment, and promoting British business overseas.

Member of Parliament for North Cotswolds (The Cotswolds 1997-2024 and for Cirencester & Tewkesbury 1992-97)

Farmer, Chartered Surveyor, and Chair of the Public Accounts Committee. Interests include the countryside, conservation, encouraging inward investment, and promoting British business overseas.

Member of Parliament for North Cotswolds (The Cotswolds 1997-2024 and for Cirencester & Tewkesbury 1992-97)

Farmer, Chartered Surveyor, and Chair of the Public Accounts Committee. Interests include the countryside, conservation, encouraging inward investment, and promoting British business overseas.

In Pictures
Visit to The Butterfly Garden
20 April 2026

Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visited The Butterfly Garden, in Bamfurlong on Friday and met with Rapid Relief Team UK and members of Men in Sheds.
Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visits Paxford Races
9 April 2026

Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visited Paxford Races near Chipping Campden on Easter Monday to support the hunt and racing.
Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown meets NFU members in Guiting Power
8 April 2026

Last week local MP Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown met with NFU members in Guiting Power to hear directly about the pressures facing local farm businesses.
News
Local Town and Parish Gloucester Fringe Infrastructure Group
2 April 2026

Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown attended the Local Town and Parish Gloucester Fringe Infrastructure Group in Quedgeley this morning.
A417 Missing Link construction site visit
31 March 2026

Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visited the A417 Missing Link construction site, where the new Project Director, Andrew Alcorn, and Assistant Project Manager, Alastair Clark gave him a drive‑through tour of the whole site.
Meeting with Bibury Heritage
30 March 2026

This morning Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown met with Bibury Heritage to discuss plans to tackle congestion in the village.
All News
In Parliament
Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown seeks reassurance on Royal Mail pension scheme transition after Capita contract termination
22 April 2026

Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown, Chair of the Public Accounts Committee, welcomes the Government statement on the administration of the Civil Service pension schemes and the decision to terminate Royal Mail Statutory Pension Scheme contract with Capita. He seeks reassurance from the Paymaster General on the future management of both schemes, and in particular on the transition arrangements for the Royal Mail scheme.
Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs calls for stronger, clearer powers to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping
20 April 2026

Speaking in a debate on the Crime and Policing Bill, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs an amendment to create a specific law enabling police to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping and he cites the success of similar police powers in combatting hare-coursing.
Clifton-Brown calls for priority heating oil support for very old and very young
16 March 2026

Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Government to ensure that the very old and the very young are given priority when allocating support to people reliant on heating oil.

Stroud District: Bisley; Hardwicke; Minchinhampton; Painswick & Upton. Tewkesbury Borough: Badgeworth; Brockworth East; Brockworth West; Churchdown Brookfield with Hucclecote; Churchdown St. John’s; Shurdington.
